articles.title: Carol Kirkwood Says Goodbye to BBC Breakfast
articles.summary: Carol Kirkwood, a weather presenter, has said an emotional goodbye to BBC Breakfast after 28 years. She told viewers it was a privilege to be in their homes and thanked them for trusting her with weather reports. Carol is leaving to travel more and spend time with her husband Steve. During her last show, she received many good wishes from friends and fans. A guide dog named Flash visited her on set, and she got gifts like a cake and a picture. Carol said she is excited to watch the weather instead of presenting it now.

articles.title: Megan Thee Stallion Gets Sick During a Show
articles.summary: Megan Thee Stallion is a famous rapper who was performing in a Broadway musical called Moulin Rouge! in New York. During her show on Tuesday night, she started feeling very ill and had to go to the hospital. Her representative said she is being checked by doctors, and they will share more news later. Megan is playing a character named Zidler, which is her first time acting on Broadway. She was excited about this new role and had just started performing in March. Her hair stylist asked people to pray for her. Megan is known for songs like Savage and has won Grammy awards. The show has had other actors play Zidler before, including a drag artist. Everyone hopes she gets better soon.

articles.title: Helicopter Pilots Visit Kid Rock's Home
articles.summary: Two Army helicopter pilots flew near Kid Rock's home in Tennessee. Kid Rock is a famous singer who supports former President Trump. He posted a video of the helicopters and saluted them. At first, the Army said the pilots would be suspended while they checked if the flight followed rules. But then, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said they could go back to work with no punishment. Kid Rock said he often sees helicopters from a nearby Army base and welcomes them. President Trump joked about it but said they probably shouldn't have done it. The Army takes flight safety seriously and will hold people accountable if needed.

articles.title: Taylor Swift Honors a Movie Star with a Music Video
articles.summary: Taylor Swift made a new music video for her song about Elizabeth Taylor, a famous actress from long ago. She did not act in the video herself. Instead, she put together clips from Elizabeth Taylor's old movies and news videos. The video shows scenes from films like Cleopatra and Cat on a Hot Tin Roof. Taylor Swift admires Elizabeth Taylor and sees her as a role model. Elizabeth Taylor was a big movie star known for her beauty and strong characters. She was also famous for her love life. The song talks about Elizabeth Taylor's life, like where she got engaged and her violet eyes. Taylor Swift got permission from Elizabeth Taylor's family to make the video. Money from the video will go to help the Elizabeth Taylor Aids Foundation. Her grandson said his grandma would have loved the song.

articles.title: Tiny DNA Robots for Medicine and Technology
articles.summary: Scientists are making tiny robots from DNA that can move inside the body. These DNA robots are like microscopic machines that can deliver medicine exactly where it's needed, such as to cancer cells or viruses. They can also help build very small data storage and computing devices. Right now, most DNA robots are still in early experiments and are not ready for real use. Researchers design them using ideas from bigger robots, like rigid parts and folding techniques similar to origami. To control their movement, scientists use special DNA sequences or outside signals like light. DNA robots could become important tools in medicine and technology in the future, but there are challenges to solve first, like making them work better in complex environments.

articles.title: Drivers Hope for Money Back from Car Loans
articles.summary: Many drivers in the UK hope to get money back because they were sold car loans unfairly. A group called the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) says about 40% of people who got car loans between 2007 and 2024 might get compensation. Poppy Whiteside from Kent is one driver who had to send many letters to her car finance company to ask for her money. She bought a Ford Fiesta in 2018 and later found out she was not told about a special commission deal that made her pay more interest. The FCA banned these deals in 2021 because they can make people pay too much. Now, drivers who were not told about such deals can claim money back. The FCA thinks 12 million people could get about £829 each. Some banks' share prices went up because fewer people than expected might get compensation.
